title: aster lingulatus
intro: Aster lingulatus, also known as the Starved Aster or Chinese Aster, is a plant species native to China and other parts of Asia, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and fern-like foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The medicinal properties of Aster lingulatus are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in the production of herbal capsules, tablets, and tinctures.
